findings.title: Floor anchors to per-period T_bars, ignoring trade sparsity — non-conservative for sparse strategies (PF4 leak the spec explicitly named)
findings.detail: v_null = 3.0/(len(returns)-1) uses the candidate's daily-bar count as the null sample size. The DSR null-dispersion of a per-period Sharpe is only ~1/T when the T bars carry ~T independent Sharpe observations; a strategy that trades rarely has an effective sample size ~n_trades, not T_bars. COVERAGE-GATE-SAFETY-MECHANISMS.md line 50 calls this out verbatim ('low-exposure cells are estimated from n_trades, not T_bars ... biasing lenient'), yet the implementation uses T_bars unconditionally and passes no n_trades in. Verified numerically at T_bars=2000: floor=3/1999=0.00150 vs effective-N null 1/n_trades = 0.20 (5 trades), 0.05 (20), 0.01 (100), 0.002 (500) — the floor is 0.008x / 0.030x / 0.15x / 0.75x of the effective null. So for anything but continuously-exposed strategies the floor sits far BELOW the true null dispersion and cannot deliver PF4's promised 'at/above realized cross-trial dispersion' conservatism — precisely in the sparse+clustered case it was built to catch. The 3.0 inflation (top of the stated 2-3x serial-dependence band; the SR^2/2 correction is negligible at per-period Sharpe, ~1.002-1.018, so 3.0 easily covers (b)'s serial+SR terms) is nowhere near enough to cover a 100-400x sparsity gap.

findings.title: Floor uses candidate's own T, but V_measured is pooled cross-trial across heterogeneous (and cross-asset) windows — incoherent scope pairing
findings.detail: The quantity being floored, trial_sr_variance, is np.var of _period_sharpe_values, which loop.py accumulates once (loop.py:761) and NEVER resets across iterations or across assets (advance_asset at loop.py:1225 keeps appending to the same list). So V_measured is a cross-trial, cross-window, cross-asset dispersion whose null expectation under zero skill is ~mean(1/T_i) over the trials' own lengths. The floor replaces it with 3/(T_candidate-1) using only the CURRENT candidate's length. When the candidate's series is longer than the typical trial (e.g. candidate on a full window, trials on shorter regime/other-asset windows), the floor collapses below even the trials' un-inflated null. Verified: T_cand=2265 vs T_trial=250 gives floor=0.00133 = 0.33x the trials' 1x null (1/250=0.004); T_cand=6000 gives 0.13x. The '3x conservative' guarantee only holds when T_candidate ~= trial T; it inverts to sub-1x otherwise. The floor and the variance it floors are not on a matched clock.

findings.title: The 'MANDATORY PF4 direction check' is reduced to a tautology; the spec's frozen-grid pre-flight measurement is absent
findings.detail: Spec (reconciled plan §2a; safety doc PF4 lines 53, 151) requires a FIXED, pre-registered V_null set 'at/above the realized cross-trial dispersion measured ONCE on the frozen grid at pre-flight', with a mandatory check sr0(N_run, V_null) >= sr0(N_run, V_status_quo_typical) on the frozen grid — an empirical guarantee against the TYPICAL status-quo hurdle. The implementation ships none of that: there is no pre-flight/frozen-grid measurement, and the only 'check' is that sr_variance_used = max(V_measured, v_null) >= V_measured, which makes sr0(used) >= sr0(measured) true by construction. test_monotone_stricter_the_mandatory_pf4_check asserts exactly this identity. It is a real per-candidate no-loosening guarantee (good), but it is not the spec's frozen-grid conservatism check and cannot detect the cases in the two HIGH findings where v_null falls below the true null. The safety property is present in name only.

findings.title: Implementation is a floor (max), not the spec's 'replace measured V' — firewall-leak claim in the safety doc is false in the non-floored regime
findings.detail: Safety doc line 151 says 'Replace the measured trial-Sharpe variance ... with a FIXED, pre-registered V_null', and line 152 asserts 'A fixed conservative null neither reads nor stores per-cell Sharpe.' The code instead does sr_variance_used = max(sr_variance, v_null): whenever the measured cross-trial dispersion exceeds v_null (dispersed trials — the common case at large N), the bar is STILL set by the measured per-cell dispersion, i.e. it still adapts to observed performance. The firewall leak PF4 was meant to close remains open in that regime. Direction is stricter-or-equal so this is not a loosening, but the doc's stated firewall property ('neither reads nor stores per-cell Sharpe') does not hold as implemented.

findings.title: Perfect clustering (V_measured == 0.0) escapes the floor via the M24 default to a lenient provisional PASS
findings.detail: The floor targets clustered trials, but the most degenerate case — bit-identical trial Sharpes, np.var == 0.0 — takes the M24 path: sr_variance<=0 -> default 0.001 -> sr_variance_defaulted=True -> is_provisional=True -> provisional PASS, regardless of the floored dsr. Verified: N=50, V=0.0 returns PASS/provisional with v_null_floored=True; N=50, V=1e-9 returns a firm floored verdict (can FAIL). So a hair's-breadth in the measured variance flips between a firm floored FAIL and a lenient provisional PASS. Pre-existing M24 behavior (the verdict was a provisional pass before this diff too), but the new floor does not close it, and it is exactly the clustering extreme the floor advertises. Separately, the floor only overrides the 0.001 default when T_bars < 3001 (crossover 3/(T-1)=0.001); on the defaulted path this only alters the reported dsr, never the verdict.

findings.title: Per-period clock is the correct co-scoping choice, but the serial-dependence inflation is hardcoded, not ValConf-derived
findings.detail: On (a): the implementation correctly floors on the per-period clock, not the spec's literal 'per-trade clock'. This is right, not wrong: V_measured (var of _period_sharpe values, loop.py:302/318), sr_hat (mean/std per-period), and sr0 are all per-period, so the floor must be per-period to be an apples-to-apples max(); following the literal 'per-trade' wording would have introduced a unit mismatch (the safety doc's own honesty note at line 151 concedes 'V, sr_hat, sr0 all per-period as the code already is'). The consequence to note: because it deviates from the spec's (1+2*sum rho_k)/T_eff construction, the serial-dependence factor is a frozen constant 3.0 rather than derived from each candidate's autocorrelation, so it is not adaptive and can be exceeded by strongly trend-persistent daily returns (sum rho_k > 1). No downstream consumer breaks: quality.py:62 uses sr_variance only for a None-check and the explicit defaulted flag; the field now carries the floored value plus a new sr_variance_measured, which is a reporting improvement.

result.summary: The PF4 floor is directionally safe — sr_variance_used = max(V_measured, v_null) guarantees sr0 >= status-quo and DSR <= status-quo, so it can never loosen the gate versus the measured variance (verified across the N=172,831 grid). The per-period clock choice is correct (co-scoped with V_measured/sr_hat, overriding the spec's loose 'per-trade' wording), and 3.0 comfortably covers both the stated 2-3x serial-dependence band and the negligible SR^2/2 term. No blocker (no wrong-direction statistics, no broken consumer). But two HIGH faithfulness gaps undermine PF4's core promise of 'conservative, at/above realized dispersion': (1) the floor anchors to per-period T_bars and ignores trade sparsity — for sparse strategies it is 0.008-0.75x the effective-N null, the exact n_trades-vs-T_bars leniency the safety doc named; (2) it uses the candidate's own T while flooring a cross-trial, cross-asset variance pooled without reset (loop.py:761), so when T_candidate exceeds the typical trial length the '3x' floor drops to 0.13-0.33x the trials' own 1x null. Two MEDIUM items: the 'mandatory direction check' is a tautology (max makes it hold by construction; the spec's frozen-grid pre-flight measurement is absent), and the max()-floor still reads per-cell measured dispersion in the non-floored regime, contradicting the safety doc's 'neither reads nor stores per-cell Sharpe' claim. Numbers verified with the repo's own DSR code.

findings.title: Floor is max(measured, v_null), not PF4's mandated fixed-V_null replacement — firewall leak persists for the common (well-dispersed) run
findings.detail: PF4 as specified (COVERAGE-GATE-SAFETY-MECHANISMS.md:151, :270, :302) is a REPLACEMENT: 'Replace the measured trial-Sharpe variance ... with a FIXED, pre-registered V_null ... assert V is never sourced from realized trial Sharpes.' The rationale is that measured cross-trial dispersion 'reads per-cell performance (firewall violation, silent vacuity lever).' The diff instead computes sr_variance_used = max(sr_variance, v_null). Whenever the MEASURED variance exceeds v_null=3/(T-1) — the common case for a genuinely dispersed search (realistic V ~3e-3..1e-2 > 1.3e-3 at T=2265) — the gate uses the MEASURED variance, i.e. V IS still sourced from realized trial Sharpes. The exact firewall violation / vacuity lever PF4 exists to remove is retained for precisely the runs where dispersion is real, and the spec's explicit test requirement (line 153: 'assert V is never sourced from realized trial Sharpes') is not met. The floor only bites the collapsed-dispersion tail. This is a design/contract deviation from the diff's own cited spec; either the code should replace (fixed V_null) per PF4, or the spec/PR framing must be corrected to 'monotone floor on measured V' and the firewall claim dropped.

findings.title: 'Mandatory PF4 check' claim is overstated: code/test assert only the trivial per-run max() inequality; the constant 3.0 is uncalibrated against typical dispersion
findings.detail: The comment says the 'mandatory PF4 check sr0(V_used) >= sr0(V_measured) holds identically' and the test test_monotone_stricter_the_mandatory_pf4_check calls this 'the pre-registered PF4 assertion.' But sr0(V_used) >= sr0(V_measured) is trivially true because V_used=max(V_measured, v_null) and sr0 is monotone in sqrt(V) — it proves nothing about the constant. The spec's actual mandatory direction check is sr0(N_run, V_null) >= sr0(N_run, V_status_quo_TYPICAL) on the frozen grid (safety doc line 54, 151), i.e. the FIXED V_null must be calibrated to sit at/above the realized cross-trial dispersion measured once on the frozen grid, and raised if it yields a lower hurdle. That calibration is absent: V_NULL_INFLATION=3.0 is a hardcoded guess (comment: 'runs 2-3x that under serial dependence'), with no Politis-White (1+2*sum rho_k)/T_eff measurement and no frozen-grid pre-flight (PF1/PF4) verifying 3/T >= realized dispersion. Consequence: if real autocorrelation pushes the null above 3/T the floor is not actually conservative (spec residual risk, safety doc line 290), and the 'verified >= status-quo hurdle' guarantee is unsubstantiated. Fix before commit: either implement the frozen-grid calibration/telemetry that backs the constant, or soften the comments/test names so they don't claim a check that isn't performed.

findings.title: Floor is materially stricter in the COMMON case, not only degenerate clustering — quantified PASS->FAIL flips near 0.95
findings.detail: Numerics (venv, T=2265 bars, v_null=3/2264=0.001325, Gaussian den~1). The floor binds for any measured V < 1.325e-3 — a large slice of the stated realistic range 1e-4..1e-2, plus ALWAYS for the 0.001 unmeasured default. For a strategy sitting exactly at DSR=0.95 under its measured V, the floored DSR becomes: V=1.2e-3 -> 0.92-0.93 (flip); V=1e-3 -> 0.85-0.89 (flip); V=7e-4 -> 0.63-0.77 (flip); V=3e-4 -> 0.19-0.47; V=1e-4 -> 0.03-0.23 (across N=20..200). The annualized-Sharpe hurdle at N=100 rises from ~0.95 (V=1e-4) or ~1.82 (V=1e-3) to ~2.01 once floored; at N=20 the V=1e-4 hurdle roughly doubles (~0.85 -> ~1.65). So the change is a large hardening across ordinary operating points, not a nudge confined to near-identical-trial clustering. This is the intended DIRECTION (monotone stricter, verified) but the MAGNITUDE is set entirely by the uncalibrated constant in the two findings above — flagging so the reviewer confirms the common-case tightening is intended and calibrated, since it reshapes acceptance broadly. Note the test suite exercises the binding regime only with synthetic V=1e-9/1e-10; the realistic-binding band V~1e-3 (where flips actually happen in production) has no test.

findings.title: n_trials<2 early-return path omits the new v_null/sr_variance_measured/v_null_floored fields
findings.detail: The floor and the v_fields dict are built after the n_trials<2 early return, so that provisional-pass path emits details with sr_variance/sr_variance_defaulted but WITHOUT v_null, sr_variance_measured, v_null_floored — while every other path includes them. Any consumer that unconditionally reads r.details['v_null_floored'] would KeyError on that path. No current consumer does (quality.py gates on 'dsr' presence, which this path omits), so it is a schema-inconsistency rather than a live break; worth making the detail payload uniform for the MON2 telemetry that is the stated purpose of these fields.

result.summary: Reviewed the uncommitted PF4 null-variance-floor diff in deflated_sharpe.py against COVERAGE-V2-RECONCILED-PLAN.md 2a and COVERAGE-GATE-SAFETY-MECHANISMS.md PF4. The statistics are directionally correct (max(V_measured, v_null) is monotone-stricter than status-quo v1: sr0 monotone in sqrt(V) => DSR_used <= DSR_measured for all N>=2; the 34 affected tests pass and I reproduced monotonicity numerically). No blocker (no wrong-direction stats, no broken consumer). CHECKS THAT CAME BACK CLEAN: (b) gate_version 1->2 is safe — gate_version is only WRITTEN to gate_results.gate_version; nothing reads/compares it for cache invalidation (gates re-run per run_id, event-sourced), so the bump is a harmless provenance stamp. (c) details['sr_variance'] now meaning USED (floored) variance does not break any consumer — quality.py reads it only for a `sr_var is None` check (still non-None), report_generator uses only n_trials, reporter.py/persistence don't touch it, and the frontend has no reference; sr_variance_measured/v_null/v_null_floored are additive. (d) provisional semantics are unchanged — the floor runs before the provisional check but never touches sr_variance_defaulted or the is_provisional logic, so a defaulted run (e.g. 0.001 floored to 0.006 at T=500) stays a provisional pass with the defaulted flag intact; only the informational DSR number shifts. THE SUBSTANTIVE ISSUES: (1, high) the diff implements a FLOOR (max of measured and null), but PF4 mandates a fixed-V_null REPLACEMENT and explicitly 'assert V is never sourced from realized trial Sharpes' — for the common well-dispersed run (measured V > v_null~1.3e-3) the gate still uses measured V, so the firewall/vacuity-lever violation PF4 exists to remove persists. (2, high) the 'mandatory PF4 check' the comment/test claim to enforce is only the trivial per-run max() inequality; the spec's real check (sr0(V_null) >= sr0(V_status_quo_typical), calibrated on the frozen grid) and the Politis-White serial-dependence inflation are not implemented — the constant 3.0 is an uncalibrated guess, so 'verified >= status-quo hurdle' is unsubstantiated and the floor may be non-conservative if real autocorrelation exceeds it. (3, medium) quantified impact: the floor binds across much of the realistic 1e-4..1e-2 variance range (and always for the 0.001 default), flipping at-threshold PASS->FAIL by large margins (DSR 0.95 -> 0.85-0.89 at V=1e-3, -> 0.03-0.23 at V=1e-4), i.e. materially stricter in the COMMON case, not just degenerate clustering — and the realistic-binding band V~1e-3 has no test (only synthetic 1e-9/1e-10). (4, low) the n_trials<2 path omits the new v_fields, a schema inconsistency. Also noted: the floor uses len(returns)-1 (per-BAR clock), which the spec calls 'per-trade' but is actually the unit-consistent choice given sr_hat is per-bar.
